description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined, olive green fuselage and broad, straight wings. The wings are adorned with roundels featuring red, white, and blue concentric circles, and the tail fin is painted with vertical red, white, and blue stripes, suggesting a national insignia. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy and is positioned just behind the propeller, indicating a single-seat configuration. The nose houses a black, three-bladed propeller, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era fighter aircraft, with a robust and aerodynamic build.
description: A green, single-propeller military-style airplane with roundel insignias on the wings and a striped tail fin, featuring an enclosed cockpit and streamlined design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three-bladed propeller, central cockpit canopy, and symmetrical wing roundels.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, wing insignia, and the colorful vertical tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the striped vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit, propeller, and wing insignia, mirroring the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
